title: Ensure log monitoring and alerts are set up for VPC network route  changes

input_row.title: Ensure log monitoring and alerts are set up for VPC network route  changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for VPC network route changes.
input_row.rationale: Routes define the paths network traffic takes from a VM instance to another destinations. The other destination can be inside your VPC network (such as another VM) or outside of it. Every route consists of a destination and a next hop. Traffic whose destination IP is within the destination range is sent to the next hop for delivery. Monitoring changes to route tables will help ensure that all VPC traffic flows through an expected path.
input_row.audit: Perform the following steps to ensure log monitoring and alerts are set for VPC network route changes. 1. Logon to SLS Console. 2. Click Log Service Audit Service in the navigation pane to go to the Log Service Audit Service page. 3. Ensure the Action Trail are Enabled under the Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration page, and click Central Project. 4. Select Alerts. 5. Ensure below alert rule has been enabled and saved in the target actiontrail_log ("event.serviceName": Ecs or "event.serviceName": Vpc) and ("event.eventName": CreateRouteEntry or "event.eventName": DeleteRouteEntry or "event.eventName": ModifyRouteEntry or "event.eventName": AssociateRouteTable or "event.eventName": UnassociateRouteTable) | select count(1) as c
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to ensure the log monitoring and alerts are set up for VPC network route changes: 1. Logon to SLS Console. 76 | P a g e 2. Click Log Service Audit Service in the navigation pane. 3. Go to Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration page. a. Select a location of project for logs. b. Check the Action Trail and configure a proper days. c. Click Save to save the changes. 4. Go to Access to Cloud Products > Global Configurations click Central Project. 5. Select Log Management > Actiontrail Log. 6. In the search/analytics console, input below query ("event.serviceName": Ecs or "event.serviceName": Vpc) and ("event.eventName": CreateRouteEntry or "event.eventName": DeleteRouteEntry or "event.eventName": ModifyRouteEntry or "event.eventName": AssociateRouteTable or "event.eventName": UnassociateRouteTable) | select count(1) as c 7. Create a dashboard and set alert for the query result.
input_row.default_value: The monitoring dashboard and alert is not set by default.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Log on to the SLS Console at the Alibaba Cloud dashboard.
2. Navigate to the "Log Service Audit Service" in the left-hand menu to access the Log Service Audit Service page.
3. Go to the "Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration" page.
   - Verify that the Action Trail is enabled.
   - Check the days configuration for logs to ensure that it meets your organization’s retention requirements.
   - Click “Save” if any changes are made.
4. Click on "Central Project" under the same access menu.
5. Go to "Log Management > Actiontrail Log."
6. In the search/analytics console, input the following query to check for VPC network route changes:
   ```
   ("event.serviceName": Ecs or "event.serviceName": Vpc) and 
   ("event.eventName": CreateRouteEntry or "event.eventName": DeleteRouteEntry or "event.eventName": ModifyRouteEntry or "event.eventName": AssociateRouteTable or "event.eventName": UnassociateRouteTable) 
   | select count(1) as c
   ```
7. Validate that an alert has been created for the query results.
8. Confirm the creation of a management dashboard that visualizes the alert for further monitoring.

**EXPECTED SECURE CONFIGURATION:**
- Action Trail must be enabled.
- Alerts for VPC route changes should be properly configured and saved.
- There should be evidence of a log monitoring dashboard.

**HOW TO CONFIRM COMPLIANCE:**
- Ensure logs are retained for the desired number of days and that the action trail is enabled.
- Verify that alerts are correctly set up to notify you of changes to VPC network routes, indicating that monitoring is in place.
